name: CJ Catrambone
initialNotes: Baseline assessment - Pre-dry fasting cycle. Blood tests available.

symptoms: Cold hands and feet turn blue: 10/10
preDryfastingCycles.notes: Pre-treatment baseline assessment. Blood test links: https://drive.google.com/u/0/open?usp=forms_web&id=14st_12dx8_8khy43UUsVtHrOH3FLF4DC; https://drive.google.com/u/0/open?usp=forms_web&id=1_ssmutkZasMX5jNJALK8Xt9nF4WBTVRq

meetings.summary: First intake meeting. Discussed extensive fasting history (max 5 dry/3 water), Sinusitis as potential dry fast target, and 'Mold Pituitary' toxicity causing Low T. Plan to start T3 and Enclomiphene.
nextSteps: Start T3 immediately (slow release from compounding, check instructions)
Start Enclomiphene (compound ~50 bucks) for Low T
Take Supplements: Antivirals (Lysine, Monolaurin, Olive Leaf), TUDCA (replace Milk Thistle)
Get DEXA scan at own clinic (once radiologist signs off)
meetings.notes:
History: 8-day fast (5 dry/3 water). Chronic Sinusitis (potential cure via '9-day dry fast' theory). 

Hormones: Low Testosterone linked to Mold/Pituitary damage. Enclomiphene chosen over TRT (fertility, shutdown concerns). Aspirin noted as anti-aromatase.

Viral/Immune: EBV/Herpes reactivation suspected. 'Vaccine' discussion (none mentioned, but 'long covid' context implied). 

Liver: Using Milk Thistle (advised to switch to TUDCA). Coffee enemas every other day (advised to stop/limit to avoid depletion).
